description: Letter fragment (lower right corner) in Judaeo-Arabic, dated Nissan 14[26?] Seleucid = 1115 CE; the last two digits of the year are not completely clear. Very little of the subject matter is discernible. The first few lines seem to be formulaic praises about yearning for the recipient. Later on: "your hands, regarding what you take out"; and "from weakness, strength"; and "God will open unto thee His good treasure." ASE.
description_he: מכתב בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
